# docker-py

{{ Build Status }}

A Python library for the Docker Remote API. It does everything the
`docker` command does, but from within Python – run containers, manage
them, pull/push images, etc.

## Installation

The latest stable version is always available on PyPi.

```
pip install docker-py
```

## Documentation

{{ Documentation Status }}

[Read the full documentation
here](https://docker-py.readthedocs.io/en/latest/). The source is
available in the `docs/` directory.

## License

Docker is licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0. See LICENSE
for full license text

modules.name: docker.types
documentation: flake8: noqa

methods.name: docker.client.Client._set_request_timeout
documentation:
Prepare the kwargs for an HTTP request by inserting the timeout
parameter, if not already present.

methods.name: docker.client.Client._raise_for_status
documentation: Raises stored {class}`APIError`, if one occurred.

methods.name: docker.client.Client._stream_helper
documentation: Generator for data coming from a chunked-encoded HTTP response.

methods.name: docker.client.Client._multiplexed_buffer_helper
documentation:
A generator of multiplexed data blocks read from a buffered
response.

methods.name: docker.client.Client._multiplexed_response_stream_helper
documentation:
A generator of multiplexed data blocks coming from a response
stream.

methods.name: docker.client.Client._stream_raw_result_old
documentation: Stream raw output for API versions below 1.6

methods.name: docker.client.Client._stream_raw_result
documentation: Stream result for TTY-enabled container above API 1.6

methods.name: docker.client.Client._disable_socket_timeout
documentation:
Depending on the combination of python version and whether we're
connecting over http or https, we might need to access _sock, which
may or may not exist; or we may need to just settimeout on socket
itself, which also may or may not have settimeout on it. To avoid
missing the correct one, we try both.

We also do not want to set the timeout if it is already disabled, as
you run the risk of changing a socket that was non-blocking to
blocking, for example when using gevent.

functions.name: docker.auth.auth.resolve_authconfig
documentation:
Returns the authentication data from the given auth configuration for a
specific registry. As with the Docker client, legacy entries in the config
with full URLs are stripped down to hostnames before checking for a match.
Returns None if no match was found.

functions.name: docker.auth.auth.parse_auth
documentation:
Parses authentication entries

Args:
  entries:        Dict of authentication entries.
  raise_on_error: If set to true, an invalid format will raise
                  InvalidConfigFile

Returns:
  Authentication registry.

functions.name: docker.auth.auth.load_config
documentation:
Loads authentication data from a Docker configuration file in the given
root directory or if config_path is passed use given path.
Lookup priority:
    explicit config_path parameter > DOCKER_CONFIG environment variable >
    ~/.docker/config.json > ~/.dockercfg

modules.name: docker.api
documentation: flake8: noqa

methods.name: docker.transport.npipeconn.NpipeHTTPConnectionPool._get_conn
documentation: When re-using connections, urllib3 tries to call select() on our
NpipeSocket instance, causing a crash. To circumvent this, we override
_get_conn, where that check happens.

modules.name: docker.transport
documentation: flake8: noqa

modules.name: docker.ssladapter.ssladapter
documentation:
Resolves OpenSSL issues in some servers:
https://lukasa.co.uk/2013/01/Choosing_SSL_Version_In_Requests/
https://github.com/kennethreitz/requests/pull/799

classes.name: docker.ssladapter.ssladapter.SSLAdapter

methods.name: docker.ssladapter.ssladapter.SSLAdapter.get_connection
documentation:
Ensure assert_hostname is set correctly on our pool

We already take care of a normal poolmanager via init_poolmanager

But we still need to take care of when there is a proxy poolmanager
An HTTPS Transport Adapter that uses an arbitrary SSL version.

classes.name: docker.transport.npipesocket.NpipeSocket
documentation:
Partial implementation of the socket API over windows named pipes.
This implementation is only designed to be used as a client socket,
and server-specific methods (bind, listen, accept...) are not
implemented.

modules.name: docker.utils
documentation: flake8: noqa

modules.name: docker.utils.types
documentation: Compatibility module. See https://github.com/docker/docker-py/issues/1196

functions.name: docker.utils.socket.read
documentation: Reads at most n bytes from socket

functions.name: docker.utils.socket.read_exactly
documentation:
Reads exactly n bytes from socket
Raises SocketError if there isn't enough data

functions.name: docker.utils.socket.next_frame_size
documentation:
Returns the size of the next frame of data waiting to be read from socket,
according to the protocol defined here:

https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/api/docker_remote_api_v1.24/#/attach-to-a-container

functions.name: docker.utils.socket.frames_iter
documentation: Returns a generator of frames read from socket

functions.name: docker.utils.utils.exclude_paths
documentation:
Given a root directory path and a list of .dockerignore patterns, return
an iterator of all paths (both regular files and directories) in the root
directory that do *not* match any of the patterns.

All paths returned are relative to the root.

functions.name: docker.utils.utils.should_include
documentation:
Given a path, a list of exclude patterns, and a list of inclusion patterns:

1. Returns True if the path doesn't match any exclusion pattern
2. Returns False if the path matches an exclusion pattern and doesn't match
   an inclusion pattern
3. Returns true if the path matches an exclusion pattern and matches an
   inclusion pattern

functions.name: docker.utils.utils.compare_version
documentation:
Compare docker versions

```pycon
>>> v1 = '1.9'
>>> v2 = '1.10'
>>> compare_version(v1, v2)
1
>>> compare_version(v2, v1)
-1
>>> compare_version(v2, v2)
0
```

functions.name: docker.utils.utils.parse_host
documentation: Based on utils.go:ParseHost http://tinyurl.com/nkahcfh
fd:// protocol unsupported (for obvious reasons)
Added support for http and https
Protocol translation: tcp -> http, unix -> http+unix

functions.name: docker.utils.utils.datetime_to_timestamp
documentation: Convert a UTC datetime to a Unix timestamp

functions.name: docker.utils.utils.parse_env_file
documentation:
Reads a line-separated environment file.
The format of each line should be "key=value".
